# PlannedWorkout Model — Comprehensive Research Report

## 1. GraphQL Schema Definition (Amplify / AppSync — Source of Truth for DDB)

**File:** `amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/schema/planned-workout.graphql` (lines 1–74)

This is the **primary schema definition** that drives the DynamoDB table structure via Amplify's `@model` directive. It defines what columns exist in DDB.

```graphql
type PlannedWorkout implements Activity @shareable @apolloKey(fields: "id")
    @model(queries: null, subscriptions: null)
    @auth(rules: [
        { allow: groups, groups: ["Admins"], operations: [create, read, update, delete] }
        { allow: private, operations: [read] }
    ]) {
    id: ID!
    versionGroupID: String! @index(name: "byVersionGroupID")
    version: Int! @default(value: "1")
    isLatest: BooleanValue! @default(value: "TRUE") @index(name: "byIsLatest")
    isPublicAndLatest: String! @index(name: "byIsPublicAndLatestAndCreatedAt", sortKeyFields: ["createdAt"])
    name: String!
    time: TimeMinutes!
    type: WorkoutType! @index(name: "byWorkoutType")
    isBodyweight: Boolean!
    estimatedDurationMillis: Int
    difficulty: Difficulty!
    instructor: Instructor @belongsTo
    bodyRegions: [BodyRegion]
    goals: [WorkoutGoal]
    description: String
    muscleGroups: [MuscleGroup!]!                    # Computed field (deprecated)
    granularMuscleGroups: [GranularMuscleGroup!]      # Computed field
    isOnMachineWorkout: Boolean
    coverImage: UserAsset
    thumbnail: UserAsset
    isChallenge: BooleanValue @index(...)
    isComplete: Boolean! @access(cognito: true) @resolver(...)   # Computed by resolver
    isGenerated: Boolean @deprecated
    isAutoGenerated: BooleanValue @index(...) @default(value: "FALSE")
    isDemoWorkout: Boolean @resolver(...)              # Computed by resolver
    workoutSource: WorkoutSource!
    originallyGenerated: Boolean @deprecated
    challengeType: ChallengeType!
    isAmpMode: BooleanValue @index(...)
    portraitThumbnail: UserAsset
    plannedBlocks: [PlannedBlock] @hasMany(...)
    categories: [WorkoutCategoryToPlannedWorkout] @hasMany(...)
    programConnections: [ProgramWorkoutConnection] @hasMany(...)
    tips: [String] @deprecated
    benefits: [String] @deprecated
    isPublic: BooleanValue! @index(...)
    createdBy: String! @index(...)
    isDraft: BooleanValue!
    isFavorite: Boolean @lambda(name: "getIsFavorite")  # Computed by Lambda
    isLiveChallenge: BooleanValue! @index(...)
    workoutGenerationVersion: String @index(...)
    aiGenerationType: AIGenerationType
    specialWorkoutType: SpecialWorkoutType @resolver(...)  # Computed by resolver
    creatorData: CreatorData @resolver(...)                 # Computed by resolver
    createdAt: AWSDateTime!
    isArchived: BooleanValue
    supportedOperations: [WorkoutOperation] @resolver(...)  # Computed by resolver
}
```

---

## 2. Database Schema / Model

**PlannedWorkout is an Amplify-managed table — it is NOT defined in `packages/amp-db/src/`.**

The DDB table is created by Amplify's `@model` directive from the schema above. There is no separate `.graphql` file in `packages/amp-db/`. The table name is registered in:

**File:** `packages/amp-backend-shared/src/utils/env/table-names.ts` (line 62)
```typescript
PlannedWorkout = 'PlannedWorkout',
```

The **build schema** (compiled by Amplify) shows the full DDB-persisted shape:

**File:** `amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/build/schema.graphql` (lines 1225–1276)

This is the resolved version after Amplify processes directives. It shows the actual DDB columns (minus computed/resolver fields):

```graphql
type PlannedWorkout implements Activity @shareable @apolloKey(fields: "id") {
  updatedAt: AWSDateTime!           # Auto-added by Amplify
  instructorPlannedWorkoutsId: ID   # Auto-added by @belongsTo
  id: ID!
  versionGroupID: String!
  version: Int!
  isLatest: BooleanValue!
  isPublicAndLatest: String!
  name: String!
  time: TimeMinutes!
  type: WorkoutType!
  isBodyweight: Boolean!

  goals: [WorkoutGoal]
  description: String

  granularMuscleGroups: [GranularMuscleGroup!]
  isOnMachineWorkout: Boolean

  isComplete: Boolean!              # Computed by resolver (not stored in DDB)
  isGenerated: Boolean

  isDemoWorkout: Boolean            # Computed by resolver
  workoutSource: WorkoutSource!
  originallyGenerated: Boolean
  challengeType: ChallengeType!
  isAmpMode: BooleanValue
  portraitThumbnail: UserAsset
  isPublic: BooleanValue!
  createdBy: String!
  isDraft: BooleanValue!
  isFavorite: Boolean               # Computed by Lambda
  isLiveChallenge: BooleanValue!
  workoutGenerationVersion: String
  aiGenerationType: AIGenerationType
  specialWorkoutType: SpecialWorkoutType  # Computed by resolver
  creatorData: CreatorData                # Computed by resolver
  createdAt: AWSDateTime!
  isArchived: BooleanValue
  isCommunityWorkout: Boolean       # Present in build schema only
  supportedOperations: [WorkoutOperation]  # Computed by resolver

```

---

## 3. Apollo Server Schema (Federation Layer)

**File:** `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/schema/planned-workout.graphql` (lines 1–55)

This is the **Apollo Server (federated subgraph)** schema. It mirrors the Amplify schema but uses federation directives (`@key`, `@override`, `@shareable`, `@unauth`) instead of Amplify directives:

```graphql
type PlannedWorkout implements Activity @key(fields: "id") @shareable @unauth {
    id: ID!
    versionGroupID: String!
    version: Int!
    isLatest: BooleanValue!
    isPublicAndLatest: String!
    name: String!
    time: TimeMinutes!
    type: WorkoutType! @override(from: "appsync")
    estimatedDurationMillis: Int
    difficulty: Difficulty!
    bodyRegions: [BodyRegion]
    isBodyweight: Boolean!
    goals: [WorkoutGoal]
    description: String
    muscleGroups: [MuscleGroup!]!
    granularMuscleGroups: [GranularMuscleGroup!]
    isOnMachineWorkout: Boolean
    coverImage: UserAsset
    thumbnail: UserAsset
    isChallenge: BooleanValue
    isComplete: Boolean!
    isFavorite: Boolean
    isGenerated: Boolean
    isAutoGenerated: BooleanValue
    workoutSource: WorkoutSource!
    originallyGenerated: Boolean @deprecated
    challengeType: ChallengeType!
    isAmpMode: BooleanValue
    portraitThumbnail: UserAsset
    calculatedPlannedBlocks(...): ModelPlannedBlockConnection
    plannedBlocks(...): ModelPlannedBlockConnection @hasMany(...) @override(from: "appsync")
    isPublic: BooleanValue!
    createdBy: String!
    isDraft: BooleanValue!
    isLiveChallenge: BooleanValue!
    workoutGenerationVersion: String
    aiGenerationType: AIGenerationType
    instructor: Instructor
    instructorPlannedWorkoutsId: ID
    creatorData: CreatorData
    specialWorkoutType: SpecialWorkoutType @override(from: "appsync")
    supportedOperations: [WorkoutOperation]
    createdAt: AWSDateTime!
    updatedAt: AWSDateTime!
    isArchived: BooleanValue
}
```

---

## 4. Shared API Types (`@amp/API`)

**File:** `packages/amp-shared/src/API.ts` (lines 7831–7888)

This is the **auto-generated TypeScript type** from codegen. All backend code imports `PlannedWorkout` from `@amp/API`:

```typescript
export type PlannedWorkout = Activity & {
  __typename?: 'PlannedWorkout';
  aiGenerationType?: Maybe<AiGenerationType>;
  benefits?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<Scalars['String']['output']>>>;

  calculatedPlannedBlocks?: Maybe<ModelPlannedBlockConnection>;
  categories?: Maybe<ModelWorkoutCategoryToPlannedWorkoutConnection>;
  challengeType: ChallengeType;
  coverImage?: Maybe<UserAsset>;
  createdAt: Scalars['AWSDateTime']['output'];
  createdBy: Scalars['String']['output'];
  creatorData?: Maybe<CreatorData>;
  description?: Maybe<Scalars['String']['output']>;
  difficulty: Difficulty;
  estimatedDurationMillis?: Maybe<Scalars['Int']['output']>;
  goals?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<WorkoutGoal>>>;
  granularMuscleGroups?: Maybe<Array<GranularMuscleGroup>>;
  id: Scalars['ID']['output'];
  instructor?: Maybe<Instructor>;
  instructorPlannedWorkoutsId?: Maybe<Scalars['ID']['output']>;
  isAmpMode?: Maybe<BooleanValue>;
  isArchived?: Maybe<BooleanValue>;
  isAutoGenerated?: Maybe<BooleanValue>;
  isBodyweight: Scalars['Boolean']['output'];
  isChallenge?: Maybe<BooleanValue>;
  isComplete: Scalars['Boolean']['output'];
  isDemoWorkout?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  isDraft: BooleanValue;
  isFavorite?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  isGenerated?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  isLatest: BooleanValue;
  isLiveChallenge: BooleanValue;
  isOnMachineWorkout?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  isPublic: BooleanValue;
  isPublicAndLatest: Scalars['String']['output'];
  muscleGroups: Array<MuscleGroup>;
  name: Scalars['String']['output'];
  originallyGenerated?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  plannedBlocks?: Maybe<ModelPlannedBlockConnection>;
  portraitThumbnail?: Maybe<UserAsset>;
  programConnections?: Maybe<ModelProgramWorkoutConnectionConnection>;
  specialWorkoutType?: Maybe<SpecialWorkoutType>;
  supportedOperations?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<WorkoutOperation>>>;
  thumbnail?: Maybe<UserAsset>;
  time: TimeMinutes;
  tips?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<Scalars['String']['output']>>>;
  type: WorkoutType;
  updatedAt: Scalars['AWSDateTime']['output'];
```

---

## 5. Boolean Field Patterns on PlannedWorkout

There are **three distinct patterns** for boolean-like fields:

### Pattern A: `BooleanValue` Enum (Stored in DDB as string "TRUE"/"FALSE")

Used when the field needs to be **indexed** or used as a **GSI hash/sort key** (DynamoDB can't index native booleans).

| Field | Required? | Default | Has Index? |
|-------|-----------|---------|------------|
| `isLatest` | `BooleanValue!` | `"TRUE"` | Yes (`byIsLatest`) |
| `isPublic` | `BooleanValue!` | — | Yes (`byIsPublicAndCreatedAt`) |
| `isDraft` | `BooleanValue!` | — | No |
| `isLiveChallenge` | `BooleanValue!` | — | Yes (`byIsLiveChallenge`) |
| `isAutoGenerated` | `BooleanValue` | `"FALSE"` | Yes (`byIsAutoGeneratedAndWorkoutType`) |
| `isChallenge` | `BooleanValue` | — | Yes (`byIsChallenge`) |
| `isAmpMode` | `BooleanValue` | — | Yes (`byIsAmpMode`) |
| `isArchived` | `BooleanValue` | — | No |

**BooleanValue enum** (defined in `amplify/.../schema/root.graphql` line 56 and `packages/amp-db/src/shared-types.graphql` line 2):
```graphql
enum BooleanValue {
    TRUE
    FALSE
}
```

**Conversion utilities** (`packages/amp-shared/src/types/boolean-value.ts`):
```typescript
booleanValueToBoolean(value: BooleanValue | null | undefined): boolean
booleanToBooleanValue(value: boolean | null | undefined): BooleanValue
```

### Pattern B: Native `Boolean` (Stored in DDB as boolean)

Used for simple boolean fields that **don't need indexing**:

| Field | Required? | Notes |
|-------|-----------|-------|
| `isBodyweight` | `Boolean!` | Stored directly in DDB |
| `isOnMachineWorkout` | `Boolean` | Optional, stored in DDB |
| `isGenerated` | `Boolean` | Deprecated |
| `originallyGenerated` | `Boolean` | Deprecated |

### Pattern C: Computed `Boolean` (NOT stored in DDB — resolved at query time)

| Field | Resolver | File |
|-------|----------|------|
| `isComplete` | Checks PerformedWorkout table | `PlannedWorkout.isComplete.resolver.ts` |
| `isFavorite` | Checks UserFavorite table | `PlannedWorkout.isFavorite.resolver.ts` |
| `isDemoWorkout` | Checks WorkoutCategory connections | AppSync VTL resolver |

---

## 6. Resolvers for PlannedWorkout

### Apollo Server Field Resolvers (computed fields)

| File | Field | Description |
|------|-------|-------------|
| `PlannedWorkout.isComplete.resolver.ts` | `isComplete` | Checks if user completed this workout |
| `PlannedWorkout.isFavorite.resolver.ts` | `isFavorite` | Checks if user favorited this workout |
| `PlannedWorkout.type.resolver.ts` | `type` | Version-gated type mapping (PILATES→MOBILITY for old clients) |
| `PlannedWorkout.specialWorkoutType.resolver.ts` | `specialWorkoutType` | Checks assessment/benchmark/welcome tables |
| `PlannedWorkout.supportedOperations.resolver.ts` | `supportedOperations` | Returns ARCHIVE/EDIT based on ownership |
| `PlannedWorkout.creatorData.resolver.ts` | `creatorData` | Fetches creator's UserProfile |
| `PlannedWorkout.instructor.resolver.ts` | `instructor` | Fetches Instructor record |
| `PlannedWorkout.plannedBlocks.resolver.ts` | `plannedBlocks` | Fetches child PlannedBlock records |
| `PlannedWorkout.calculatedPlannedBlocks.resolver.ts` | `calculatedPlannedBlocks` | Fetches blocks with participant data |

### Query/Mutation Resolvers

| File | Operation |
|------|-----------|
| `Query.getPlannedWorkout.resolver.ts` | Get single workout by ID |
| `Query.queryPlannedWorkouts.resolver.ts` | Search/filter workouts |
| `Query.listPersonalPlannedWorkouts.resolver.ts` | List user's personal workouts |
| `Mutation.generatePlannedWorkoutV3.resolver.ts` | V3 workout generation |
| `Mutation.generatePlannedWorkoutForUserV2.resolver.ts` | V2 workout generation |
| `Mutation.applyQuickActionPlannedWorkout.resolver.ts` | Quick action modifications |
| `Mutation.applyAIChangesToPlannedWorkout.resolver.ts` | AI-driven modifications |
| `Mutation.createPlannedWorkoutFull.resolver.ts` | Create workout (admin) |
| `Mutation.updatePlannedWorkoutFull.resolver.ts` | Update workout (admin) |
| `Mutation.createPersonalPlannedWorkoutFull.resolver.ts` | Create personal workout |
| `Mutation.updatePersonalPlannedWorkoutFull.resolver.ts` | Update personal workout |
| `Mutation.toggleArchived.resolver.ts` | Toggle isArchived field |
| `Mutation.toggleFavorite.resolver.ts` | Toggle favorite status |

---

## 7. How to Add a New Boolean Field — Step-by-Step Pattern

Based on the `isArchived` field (the most recent addition), here's the pattern:

### If the field is **stored in DDB** and **doesn't need indexing**:

1. **Amplify schema** (`amplify/.../schema/planned-workout.graphql`):
   - Add `myNewField: Boolean` (optional) or `myNewField: Boolean!` (required)
   - If it needs a GSI, use `BooleanValue` instead of `Boolean`

2. **Apollo Server schema** (`packages/amp-api/apolloServer/schema/planned-workout.graphql`):
   - Add the same field to the `PlannedWorkout` type

3. **Run codegen**: `pnpm generate` (which runs `pnpm gql-compile && pnpm cf2tf && pnpm codegen`)
   - This regenerates `packages/amp-shared/src/API.ts` with the new field
   - Also regenerates the supergraph schema and router schemas

4. **If the field needs a resolver** (computed, not stored):
   - Create `PlannedWorkout.myNewField.resolver.ts` in `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/src/resolvers/`
   - Follow the pattern from `isComplete` or `isFavorite` resolvers

5. **If the field needs write support**:
   - Use `patchPlannedWorkoutField()` from `packages/amp-backend-shared/src/models/planned-workout/patch-planned-workout.ts`
   - For `BooleanValue` fields, use `booleanToBooleanValue()` from `@amp/shared/types/boolean-value`

### If the field uses `BooleanValue` enum (for indexing):

Same as above, but:
- Use `BooleanValue` type instead of `Boolean`
- Add `@default(value: "FALSE")` if needed
- Add `@index(name: "byMyField")` if you need a GSI
- In TypeScript, the field type will be `Maybe<BooleanValue>` (optional) or `BooleanValue` (required)

---

## 8. Key Utility Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `packages/amp-shared/src/types/boolean-value.ts` | `booleanToBooleanValue()` / `booleanValueToBoolean()` |
| `packages/amp-backend-shared/src/models/planned-workout/patch-planned-workout.ts` | `patchPlannedWorkoutField()` — generic DDB patch |
| `packages/amp-backend-shared/src/models/planned-workout/get-planned-workout.ts` | `getPlannedWorkoutById()` and query helpers |
| `packages/amp-backend-shared/src/models/planned-workout/duplicate-planned-workout.ts` | Full workout duplication with all blocks/movements |
| `packages/amp-backend-shared/src/features/generate-workout-v2/generators/workout-generator-service.ts` | `saveWorkoutToDb()` — main save path |
| `packages/amp-backend-shared/src/utils/env/table-names.ts` | `TableName.PlannedWorkout` enum |

---

## 9. Migration / Codegen Steps Summary

| Step | Command | What it does |
|------|---------|-------------|
| 1 | Edit `.graphql` files | Add field to Amplify + Apollo schemas |
| 2 | `pnpm generate` | Full codegen: compiles GQL → generates API types → generates CF→TF → generates DB types |
| 3 | `pnpm typecheck` | Verify TypeScript compiles |
| 4 | `pnpm lint` | Verify lint passes |

**Important**: PlannedWorkout is Amplify-managed, so there is **no `pnpm codegen:db`** step needed (that's only for `packages/amp-db` tables). The Amplify `@model` directive handles the DDB table definition. The `pnpm generate` command handles everything.

**No DDB migration needed**: DynamoDB is schemaless. Adding a new optional field to the GraphQL schema just means new records will have the field, and old records will return `null` for it. No table migration is required.

- `GetWorkoutTogether($id: ID!, $workoutParticipantIds: [ID!])` → uses `PlannedWorkoutFullWithArguments`

---

### 4. Workout Details Screen Architecture

**📁 `Amp/UI/Screens/Library/WorkoutDetails/`**

**4a. ViewModel** (`WorkoutDetailsViewModel.swift`)
- Context enum: `.id(String)`, `.workout(PlannedWorkout)`, `.fullWorkout(PlannedWorkout)`, `.aiWorkout(...)`, `.quickActionWorkout(...)`, `.aiQuickActionWorkout(...)`
- Uses `LibraryAPIProtocol` to load minimal → full workout
- State: `Loadable<WorkoutDetailsViewData>`
- Nav bar: close, share, favorite, edit; Bottom: `WorkoutDetailsStartWorkoutButton`

**4b. ViewData** (`WorkoutDetailsViewData.swift`)
```swift
struct WorkoutDetailsViewData: Equatable {
    let items: [WorkoutDetailsItem]
    let title: String?
    let isEditEnabled: Bool
}
```

**4c. Item Enum** (`Views/ViewController/WorkoutDetailsItem.swift`)
```swift
enum WorkoutDetailsItem {
    case header
    case title(WorkoutDetailsTitleCellViewData)
    case voiceMessage
    case quickActions
    case description(String)
    case creator(WorkoutDetailsCreatorSectionViewData, Bool, Bool)
    case workoutPlanHeader(Int, WorkoutDetailsPlanBlockHeaderCellViewData)
    case workoutPlanItem(WorkoutPlanMovementViewData, Bool)
    case metadata(WorkoutDetailsMetadataCellViewData)
    case muscles(MusclesViewData)
    case archive
}
```

**4d. Section Type** (`Views/ViewController/WorkoutDetailsSectionType.swift`)
```swift
enum WorkoutDetailsSectionType: CaseIterable { case main }
```

**4e. State Mapper — Item Order** (`WorkoutDetailsStateMapper.swift`, 157 lines)

Items built in order:
1. `.header` — always
2. `.title(titleViewData)` — name (truncated 28 chars) + first 3 unique muscle groups
3. `.voiceMessage` — if hasVoiceViewModel && isVoiceMessageVisible
4. `.creator(...)` — if not challenge and no voice message
5. `.description(...)` — voice message text or workout description
6. `.quickActions` — if not preparing and quick actions enabled
7. `.muscles(...)` — if muscleGroups not empty
8. Workout plan blocks (if not challenge): `.workoutPlanHeader` + `.workoutPlanItem` per movement
9. `.archive` — if supportedOperations contains `.archive`

**4f. Title Cell View Data** (`Views/ViewController/Cells/ViewData/WorkoutDetailsTitleCellViewData.swift`)
```swift

    let mainMuscleGroups: [Graph.MuscleGroup]
    let name: String
}
```

Excellent — the research is very thorough. Key finding: **`isCommunityWorkout` already exists in the iOS codebase** (in the Swift model, GraphQL fragments, header view data, badge style `.community`, and `LibraryBadgesView`). It also appears in the backend's build schema. However, it's **missing from the source Amplify schema and the Apollo Server schema**. This means the iOS side was prepared for this field but the backend never actually added it to the source schemas.

Let me verify this quickly.

The iOS app is **already fully implemented** — the `isCommunityWorkout` field exists in:
